incorrects.description: Student selected 2: likely counting only some containers or misinterpreting the visual

params.description: All three bags highlight one at a time.

steps.text: Count each bag separately. How many bags do you see in total?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4: counting items per bag instead of number of bags

params.description: The three bags themselves highlight, not the items inside.

steps.text: You counted the items in each bag. The question asks how many groups. Count the bags themselves.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6: likely doubling or misunderstanding the counting structure

params.description: The three bags pulse to emphasize their count.

steps.text: A group is one bag with all its items inside. Count just the bags. How many do you see?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Each bag highlights in sequence as the system counts them: 1, 2, 3.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags one by one. First bag, second bag, third bag. That's 3 groups. Each bag is one group, no matter how many items are inside.
